name: Elasticsearch (Docker)
description: Connect to Elasticsearch clusters via Docker. List indices, inspect field mappings, and run full-text and query-DSL searches over your data, in an isolated container.

inputs.label: Elasticsearch URL
inputs.description: The URL of your Elasticsearch cluster. Use host.docker.internal instead of localhost to reach a local cluster from inside the container (e.g., https://host.docker.internal:9200).

inputs.label: Elasticsearch API Key
inputs.description: An Elasticsearch API key used to authenticate to the cluster. Create one in Kibana under Stack Management > Security > API keys, or via the Elastic Cloud console.

obtain.instructions:
1. Open Kibana for your deployment
2. Go to Stack Management > Security > API keys
3. Click Create API key and set the name and privileges
4. Copy the encoded API key value

Elastic Cloud: open your deployment in the Elastic Cloud console to find Kibana and the Elasticsearch endpoint URL.

auth.instructions: Authenticate with an Elasticsearch API key (ES_API_KEY). Basic auth (ES_USERNAME + ES_PASSWORD) is also supported by the server as an alternative. Create an API key in Kibana under Stack Management > Security > API keys.
